title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-18 · 美伊新协议与2015年JCPOA对比
summary: CNN《今晨》节目讨论特朗普与伊朗签署的结束战争协议。共和党人批评该协议是"美国投降"和"外交政策失误"，认为伊朗更强而盟友更弱。协议允许伊朗保留60%浓缩铀并在当地降级至3.67%，而非像2015年协议那样运出。以色列要求零浓缩，但协议未实现。特朗普在Truth Social上反击批评者。节目还涉及纽约市长确认泰勒和特拉维斯婚礼日期、华盛顿特区市长初选社会主义者领先等话题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.en: Now that we've all seen the agreement President Trump signed with Iran, you have to ask, was it worth going to war over? SEN. BILL CASSIDY (R-LA): This will go down as a tremendous foreign policy blunder.
paragraphs.cn: 既然我们都看到了特朗普总统与伊朗签署的协议，你不得不问，这值得为此开战吗？参议员比尔·卡西迪（路易斯安那州共和党人）：这将被视为一个巨大的外交政策失误。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And Vice President Vance better look out. The bus could be coming around the corner.
paragraphs.cn: 万斯副总统最好小心点。巴士可能正从拐角处驶来。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: This way, if it works out, I'm going to take the credit. If it doesn't work out, I'm blaming J.D. You better be careful,
paragraphs.cn: 这样一来，如果成功了，我会居功。如果失败了，我就怪J.D.。你最好小心点。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Cornish
paragraphs.en: And the world champion New York Knicks get an invite to the White House. But will any of the players boycott? In the meantime, World Cup fans are booing water breaks as a vibe killer that makes the games too -- I don't know -- American. So, is FIFA all wet?
paragraphs.cn: 世界冠军纽约尼克斯队受邀前往白宫。但会有球员抵制吗？与此同时，世界杯球迷们对补水暂停发出嘘声，认为这破坏了比赛节奏，让比赛变得太 -- -- 怎么说呢 -- -- 美国化。那么，国际足联是不是全湿了？

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: This was not easy, I can tell you.
paragraphs.cn: 我可以告诉你，这并不容易。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, signed on the dotted line. The U.S. and Iran have both signed an agreement to end the war on all fronts, for better or worse, at least for the U.S. Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with the details of that agreement, but also the Republican response. Conservative voices online, not pleased about what's in the text of this agreement, some calling it an American surrender, saying it's even worse than the Obama deal of 2015.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，在虚线上签了字。美国和伊朗都签署了一项协议，以结束所有战线上的战争，无论好坏，至少对美国来说是这样。大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。我们将从该协议的细节开始，但也会关注共和党的反应。网上的保守派声音对协议文本的内容并不满意，有些人称其为美国的投降，甚至比2015年的奥巴马协议还要糟糕。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, Nikki Haley called it a, quote, "huge mistake to pay to rebuild the threat we just destroyed." Senator Ted Cruz told "The Daily Wire" he felt like Trump was getting very poor advice and called it ill-advised. In the meantime, Senator Bill Cassidy took it one step further.
paragraphs.cn: 妮基·黑利称其为"花钱重建我们刚刚摧毁的威胁的巨大错误"。参议员特德·克鲁兹告诉《每日电讯报》，他觉得特朗普得到了非常糟糕的建议，并称其不明智。与此同时，参议员比尔·卡西迪更进一步。

paragraphs.speaker: Cassidy
paragraphs.en: This will go down as a tremendous foreign policy blunder. Iran ends up stronger. Our allies in the region are weaker, and Iran has learned that, if they're willing to grab that Strait of Hormuz and choke it off, they can get the Western world to dance to their tune. I think it's a deep mistake.
paragraphs.cn: 这将被视为一个巨大的外交政策失误。伊朗最终变得更强大。我们在该地区的盟友更弱了，而伊朗已经学到，如果他们愿意抓住霍尔木兹海峡并扼住它，他们就能让西方世界跟着他们的节奏跳舞。我认为这是一个严重的错误。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today in the group chat, Nicholas Wu, congressional reporter at "Semafor"; Sina Azodi, director of Middle East studies programs at George Washington University; Mike Dubke, former Trump White House communications director; and Meghan Hays, former Biden White House director of message planning. So, I want to start with you, Sina, because you've been watching this very closely. And one point interested me.
paragraphs.cn: 今天在群聊中，有《Semafor》的国会记者尼古拉斯·吴；乔治华盛顿大学中东研究项目主任西纳·阿佐迪；前特朗普白宫通讯主任迈克·杜布克；以及前拜登白宫信息规划主任梅根·海斯。所以，我想先问你，西纳，因为你一直在密切关注此事。有一点让我很感兴趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: In the Obama deal, basically, the plan was you ship out all your enriched uranium, right? Iran had about, it says, 20 percent enriched. And they would send that somewhere. It ended up being Russia. But in this deal -- in this deal, it's a little different. Basically, the stockpile, which at this point is at 60 percent, is kept inside Iran, and then will be down-blended locally. But it means they keep it on their soil. Is this one of the aspects of the deal that has people really upset?
paragraphs.cn: 在奥巴马协议中，基本上计划是你把所有浓缩铀运出去，对吧？伊朗当时有大约20%的浓缩铀。他们会将其送到某个地方，最终是俄罗斯。但在这个协议中 -- -- 在这个协议中，情况有点不同。基本上，目前达到60%的库存留在伊朗境内，然后在当地进行降级混合。但这意味着他们将其保留在自己的土地上。这是协议中让人们非常不满的方面之一吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Sina Azodi
paragraphs.en: Well, what the JCPOA stipulated was that Iran would ship out 98 percent of its enriched uranium to Russia. Right.
paragraphs.cn: 《联合全面行动计划》规定，伊朗将把其98%的浓缩铀运往俄罗斯。没错。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It leaves the country.
paragraphs.cn: 这些浓缩铀将离开伊朗。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: Leaves the country. In this case, I think for whatever reason -- it's distrust, the possibility of the U.S. leaving the deal again -- Iranians have insisted that it has to stay on their soil. But to, let's say, mitigate the concerns for proliferation, they're going to down-blend it to 3.67 percent, which is low enriched uranium. And you use that for a nuclear power plant.
paragraphs.cn: 离开伊朗。但在目前情况下，我认为出于某种原因 -- -- 不信任、美国可能再次退出协议 -- -- 伊朗坚持这些浓缩铀必须留在其境内。但为了缓解对核扩散的担忧，他们将把浓缩铀的丰度降低到3.67%，即低浓缩铀，用于核电站。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: Doesn't have any weaponization concerns.
paragraphs.cn: 这不存在武器化的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, but different from one leaves. One stays, basically.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但这与离开伊朗不同。基本上，这些浓缩铀留在了伊朗。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And the administration is saying, look, this -- this is a good solution, because it's going to get us to zero. But it leads to my other point. It's not -- it's non-zero, not zero.
paragraphs.cn: 而美国政府表示，这是一个好的解决方案，因为它将使我们达到零。但这引出了我的另一个观点。这不是零，而是非零。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And I know Israel said, look, there should be zero. No enrichment whatsoever. Nothing enriched anywhere. But that is not what this deal does.
paragraphs.cn: 我知道以色列说过，应该是零。不允许任何浓缩活动。任何地方都不能有浓缩铀。但这项协议并没有做到这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: No. And the problem -- and we've been dealing with this issue of the principal of enrichment in Iran for more than 20 years now. Iran's interpretation, or the legal interpretation of the NPT, the article four of the NPT, their interpretation is that that right to use peaceful energy includes enrichment. The U.S. side says, no, absolutely not. The text of the NPT doesn't say enrichment. So, they've been fighting over this on this legal interpretation.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。问题在于 -- -- 我们处理伊朗浓缩铀原则问题已经超过20年了。伊朗对《不扩散核武器条约》第四条的解释是，和平利用核能的权利包括浓缩铀。而美方则坚决反对，因为条约文本并未提及浓缩铀。因此，双方一直就这一法律解释争论不休。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And that fight hasn't changed? Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 这场争论没有改变？是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: Hasn't changed -- changed. What President Obama did was that he said the enrichment in Iran is a fact on the ground. We can't change it. President Trump said, no, it's a bad deal. Withdrew. And we are back to 2018 now.
paragraphs.cn: 没有改变。奥巴马总统的做法是承认伊朗的浓缩铀是既成事实，我们无法改变。特朗普总统则认为这是一项糟糕的协议，并退出了。现在我们又回到了2018年的状态。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. So, the reason why I wanted to set the table with all that is, like, why would all these conservative voices be squawking about this? Right? Why would the Lindsey Grahams of the world, who were all too happy to be a part of these strikes and have these demands on Iran, be so frustrated with what was signed? Nicholas, what's your sense in Congress about, A, why they feel comfortable saying it out loud and why they're saying what they're saying? NICHOLAS WU, CONGRESSIONAL REPORTER, "SEMAFOR": Well, for
paragraphs.cn: 是的。所以，我想摆出所有这些事实的原因是，为什么这些保守派声音会对此大惊小怪？对吧？为什么像林赛·格雷厄姆这样的人，他们曾非常乐意参与这些打击行动并对伊朗提出这些要求，现在却对签署的协议如此不满？尼古拉斯，你在国会的感觉如何？第一，为什么他们觉得可以公开说出来；第二，他们为什么这么说？《Semafor》国会记者尼古拉斯·吴：嗯，对于

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Republicans on Capitol Hill that I've talked to, I mean, this strikes at the very heart of the issue for them; like, the issue of Iran's nuclear program, the reasons they went to war. This all kind of comes to a head now, because this deal and what's emerging, by all accounts, in the view of these Republicans, didn't accomplish that goal.
paragraphs.cn: 我交谈过的国会山共和党人来说，这触及了他们问题的核心；比如，伊朗核计划问题，他们发动战争的原因。现在这一切都到了紧要关头，因为根据所有说法，在这群共和党人看来，这项协议及其产生的结果并没有实现那个目标。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And so, for these Republicans who, you know, see this as a deeply held principle for them and know that they will outlast President Trump, they're willing to speak up about it now.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，对于这些共和党人来说，他们视此为一项根深蒂固的原则，并且知道他们会比特朗普总统更长久，所以他们现在愿意直言不讳。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. It's interesting. As we are speaking, Mike Dubke --
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Ini menarik. Saat kita bicara, Mike Dubke -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- your favorite person just posted to Truth Social. President Trump says, look, "These fools who think I haven't been tough enough on Iran when the Stock Market Just Hit a RECORD HIGH, and Oil prices are 'tumbling' down, are either jealous, bad people or stupid." So, let's talk messaging.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- orang favoritmu baru saja memposting di Truth Social. Presiden Trump berkata, lihat, "Orang-orang bodoh ini pikir saya tidak cukup keras terhadap Iran, dan pasar saham baru saja mencapai rekor tertinggi, harga minyak sedang 'jatuh', mereka iri, jahat, atau bodoh." Jadi, mari kita bicara tentang penyampaian pesan.

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: Well, first of all, this is just a framework. This isn't the total deal. So, we need to keep that in mind. I mean, we keep -- all the rhetoric I keep hearing is, you know, this deal, this deal, that. It's a framework to get to a deal.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, pertama, ini hanya kerangka kerja. Bukan kesepakatan penuh. Jadi, kita perlu ingat itu. Maksudku, kita terus -- -- yang selalu saya dengar adalah, Anda tahu, kesepakatan ini, kesepakatan itu. Ini hanya kerangka kerja untuk mencapai kesepakatan.

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: So, we can argue whether or not that's great or not. And I appreciate the -- the laughter on my left. The other thing is when you're in a hole, what's the first rule? Stop digging. And I think that's where we are with this particular framework, this particular MOU, is that we are at a point. The military in Iran has been degraded, whether or not it's been blasted to the Stone Age. Again, we can debate that. The ballistic missile stockpile has been degraded. Their ability to fund Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis --
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, kita bisa berdebat apakah ini baik atau buruk. Saya hargai tawa di sebelah kiri saya. Hal lain adalah, ketika Anda dalam masalah, aturan pertama apa? Berhenti menggali. Saya pikir itulah posisi kita dalam kerangka kerja khusus ini, nota kesepahaman khusus ini, kita berada di titik tertentu. Kekuatan militer Iran telah dilemahkan, apakah dibom kembali ke Zaman Batu atau tidak. Sekali lagi, kita bisa berdebat. Persediaan rudal balistik telah dilemahkan. Kemampuan mereka mendanai Hamas, Hizbullah, dan Houthi -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And we should say --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, kita harus mengatakan -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: -- has been degraded.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- telah dilemahkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- Europe, NATO. Marc Rich (ph) was speaking earlier. I don't know if that was sound. But they're not saying, Oh, God, this is really bad. Right? We heard Carney yesterday say, I like what I saw.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- Eropa, NATO. Mark Rich berbicara lebih awal. Saya tidak tahu apakah itu suaranya. Tapi mereka tidak berkata, oh astaga, ini benar-benar buruk. Kan? Kemarin kami dengar Carney berkata, saya suka apa yang saya lihat.

paragraphs.speaker: Meghan Hays
paragraphs.en: Well, what are they supposed to do? They're standing with him. They're not going to say anything in front of him. First of all, we spent $1 billion a day fighting a war that no one wanted to go to. So, we spent over $100 billion there. We spent $300 billion in this deal. So, now we're $400 billion when Americans don't have healthcare.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, apa yang harus mereka lakukan? Mereka mendukungnya. Mereka tidak akan berkata apa-apa di depannya. Pertama, kita menghabiskan $1 miliar sehari untuk perang yang tidak ingin dimenangkan siapa pun. Kita menghabiskan lebih dari $100 miliar di sana. Kita menghabiskan $300 miliar dalam kesepakatan ini. Jadi, sekarang kita telah menghabiskan $400 miliar, dan rakyat Amerika tidak punya jaminan kesehatan.

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: We did not spend $300 billion in this deal. If --
paragraphs.cn: Kita tidak menghabiskan $300 miliar dalam kesepakatan ini. Jika -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: You tell me, then --
paragraphs.cn: Maka katakan padaku -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: I just can't lay that. I just can't let you just get away with that.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak bisa membiarkanmu lolos begitu saja. Saya tidak bisa membiarkanmu melenggang begitu saja.

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Let me give the fact check. There will be a $300 billion --
paragraphs.cn: Baik. Biarkan saya periksa faktanya. Akan ada $300 miliar -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- investment fund. But a lot of people say, why should Iran have almost immediate access to investments in the world economy if it's still sitting on its enriched uranium and so much of this is not worked out yet?
paragraphs.cn: -- -- dana investasi. Tapi banyak orang berkata, jika Iran masih memiliki uranium yang diperkaya, dan banyak masalah belum terselesaikan, mengapa Iran harus segera mendapatkan investasi ke ekonomi dunia?

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: I would say that a possibility of instability in Iran is a danger to everybody, the entire region.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir ketidakstabilan Iran bisa berbahaya bagi semua orang, seluruh kawasan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, you think the money is necessary to restabilize?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, Anda pikir uang ini diperlukan untuk menstabilkan kembali?

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: To restabilize the country. And I think that it -- you know, just looking at President Trump's approach, investment, it could work for the Americans, too. A lot of jobs. Think of it: in 2015, Iran signed an agreement with Boeing to buy passenger planes, which never happened, because President Trump decided to leave.
paragraphs.cn: Untuk menstabilkan kembali negara itu. Dan saya pikir, Anda tahu, lihat apa yang dilakukan Presiden Trump, investasi juga bisa menguntungkan AS. Banyak lapangan kerja. Pikirkan: tahun 2015, Iran menandatangani kesepakatan dengan Boeing untuk membeli pesawat, tapi tidak pernah terwujud karena Presiden Trump memutuskan untuk keluar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But you can hear why critics would say, Well, wait a second. Now were talking about investment with Iran, with a regime that fundamentally is not changed.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi Anda bisa mengerti mengapa para kritikus berkata, tunggu, sekarang kita bicara tentang investasi dengan Iran, dengan rezim yang tidak berubah sama sekali.

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: Exactly my point. So now, Americans are using -- the American taxpayers are paying for something that we never -- a war we didn't want to go to. Gas prices shot up, and got a huge mess to our global economy, not to mention our own economy here. And Trump doesn't seem to care. He just is like, Oh, if you're stupid, you don't like this. Well, they still have -- they still have the capability for nuclear power -- or nuclear war capabilities.
paragraphs.cn: Itulah poin saya. Jadi sekarang, rakyat Amerika menggunakan -- -- pembayar pajak Amerika membiayai perang yang tidak pernah ingin kami ikuti. Harga minyak melonjak, menyebabkan kekacauan besar bagi ekonomi global, apalagi ekonomi kita sendiri. Dan Trump sepertinya tidak peduli. Dia seperti berkata, oh, jika Anda bodoh, Anda tidak akan suka ini. Yah, mereka masih memiliki nuklir -- -- atau kemampuan perang nuklir.

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: This is an agreement to -- to a deal, which this is the 39th time he said that we almost have a deal. So, at least we're one step closer there. But I just don't understand how we are in this position that -- in a war that no one wanted to go to, and the American people are the ones suffering.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah kesepakatan -- -- sebuah transaksi, ini adalah ke-39 kalinya dia bilang kita hampir mencapai kesepakatan. Jadi, setidaknya kita selangkah lebih dekat. Tapi saya tidak mengerti, bagaimana kita bisa terlibat dalam perang yang tidak ingin dimenangkan siapa pun, dan yang menderita adalah rakyat Amerika.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me give Sina the last word, because he's here. And this was signed at Versailles. Lots of things have been signed at Versailles.
paragraphs.cn: Biarkan saya memberi Sena kesempatan terakhir untuk bicara, karena dia di sini. Ini ditandatangani di Versailles. Banyak kesepakatan ditandatangani di Versailles.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But usually, when you call something a Versailles treaty, it's in foreign language policy land. Kind of an insult, right? It's a self-defeating agreement. What's your response to the critics out there who are making those analogies?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi biasanya, ketika Anda menyebut sesuatu sebagai Perjanjian Versailles, itu dalam kebijakan luar negeri. Agak menghina, kan? Itu adalah kesepakatan yang merugikan diri sendiri. Apa tanggapan Anda terhadap kritikus yang membuat analogi itu?

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: Look, I mean, absolutely what Meghan said, I mean, this was the -- President Trump didn't have to sign that peace deal at Versailles today. He could have had an agreement in February before he decided to go to war. He was dragged into a war of choice that he didn't have to. Thirteen Americans died. Billions of dollars spent. He could have taken the deal that the Iranians had offered, and it was a pretty good deal compared to the JCPOA. And I know that President Trump is --
paragraphs.cn: Dengar, saya sepenuhnya setuju dengan Megan. Presiden Trump tidak harus menandatangani perjanjian damai itu di Versailles hari ini. Dia bisa saja mencapai kesepakatan sebelum memutuskan perang pada bulan Februari. Dia terseret ke dalam perang pilihan yang tidak perlu. 13 orang Amerika tewas, menghabiskan miliaran dolar. Dia bisa saja menerima tawaran Iran, dan itu adalah kesepakatan yang cukup bagus dibandingkan dengan JCPOA. Saya tahu Presiden Trump -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Did not believe that.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak percaya itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: -- very sensitive to the word "JCPOA" and Obama.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- sangat sensitif terhadap kata "JCPOA" dan Obama.

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: But that was a very good deal that he had on February 26 in Geneva.
paragraphs.cn: But that was a very good deal he got in Geneva on February 26.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, the hope is -- from the White House that 60 days from now, whatever they have will be much better --
paragraphs.cn: Well, the White House hopes that after 60 days, whatever they get will be much better than what happened in 2015 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- than happened in 2015. Thank you so much for your expertise. I hope you can come back.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- better than what happened in 2015. Thank you very much for your expert insights. Hope you can come again.

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.en: Thank you for having me.
paragraphs.cn: Thank you for the invitation.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, there's a new release date for Sean "Diddy" Combs. What this means for his time behind bars. And Swifties, did New York's mayor just let a secret slip?
paragraphs.cn: On CNN This Morning, Sean "Diddy" Combs has a new release date. What that means for his prison time. And, Swifties, did the New York mayor accidentally leak a secret?
